verses.text: The LORD: if a woman bears a male, she is unclean seven days. The eighth day his foreskin is circumcised. She continues in the blood of purifying thirty-three days; touches no holy thing till fulfilled. If a female — unclean two weeks; sixty-six days of purifying. Guidance: Childbirth brings a woman near the boundary of life and death. Forty days for a boy, eighty for a girl reflect the body's healing, not the child's worth. Mary kept this very law in Luke 2.

She continues in the blood of purifying thirty-three days; touches no holy thing till fulfilled. If a female — unclean two weeks; sixty-six days of purifying. Guidance: Childbirth brings a woman near the boundary of life and death. Forty days for a boy, eighty for a girl reflect the body's healing, not the child's worth. Mary kept this very law in Luke 2.

The LORD: if a woman bears a male, she is unclean seven days. The eighth day his foreskin is circumcised. She continues in the blood of purifying thirty-three days; touches no holy thing till fulfilled. If a female — unclean two weeks; sixty-six days of purifying. Guidance: Childbirth brings a woman near the boundary of life and death. Forty days for a boy, eighty for a girl reflect the body's healing, not the child's worth. Mary kept this very law in Luke 2.

The LORD: if a woman bears a male, she is unclean seven days. The eighth day his foreskin is circumcised. She continues in the blood of purifying thirty-three days; touches no holy thing till fulfilled. If a female — unclean two weeks; sixty-six days of purifying. Guidance: Childbirth brings a woman near the boundary of life and death. Forty days for a boy, eighty for a girl reflect the body's healing, not the child's worth. Mary kept this very law in Luke 2.

The LORD: if a woman bears a male, she is unclean seven days. The eighth day his foreskin is circumcised. She continues in the blood of purifying thirty-three days; touches no holy thing till fulfilled. If a female — unclean two weeks; sixty-six days of purifying. Guidance: Childbirth brings a woman near the boundary of life and death. Forty days for a boy, eighty for a girl reflect the body's healing, not the child's worth. Mary kept this very law in Luke 2.

When the days of purifying are fulfilled, she brings a lamb for burnt offering, a young pigeon or turtledove for sin offering. The priest offers, atones, she is cleansed. If she cannot bring a lamb — two turtledoves or two young pigeons. The priest atones; she is clean. Guidance: A woman comes back into the sanctuary on her own — bringing her own sacrifice. Joseph and Mary brought the two birds at Luke 2:24 — a poor mother's offering for the Son of God.
